title: How Goldmine Scores AI Tools Before They Go Mainstream
excerpt: Most AI tool lists are curated by humans with opinions. Goldmine uses real momentum data from 6 platforms to surface what's actually gaining traction, before the hype cycle kicks in.

title: Why ThothAI Processes Everything on Your Device
excerpt: Cloud AI is convenient until it isn't. ThothAI runs entirely on your device, no servers, no accounts, no data leaving your machine. Here's why we built it that way.

title: What Is RAG and Why It Matters for Your Documents
excerpt: Retrieval-Augmented Generation sounds complicated, but the idea is simple: instead of guessing, the AI searches your actual documents first. Here's how it works and why it's better.

title: The 6 Platforms We Monitor to Find AI Tools Early
excerpt: GitHub, Hacker News, Product Hunt, Reddit, arXiv, and Dev.to, each one tells a different story about whether an AI tool is real or just noise. Here's what we look for on each.

title: 70+ GitHub Repos in a Year. No CS Degree. Here's How.
excerpt: Thirty years in IT, zero formal dev background, and 70+ repos shipped in about a year. The honest story of what changed when I started building with AI, and why domain knowledge matters more than knowing how to code.

title: Why Every App I Build Has Zero Telemetry and One-Time Pricing
excerpt: No accounts, no subscriptions, no data leaving your device. Why every app I ship is local-first and one-time purchase, a decision I made early and haven't changed.

title: Stop Vibing and Write the Doc First
excerpt: Pure vibes coding works right up until it doesn't. How writing a simple PRD and a CLAUDE.md before any code stopped me throwing away half my builds, and kept the AI from drifting off-scope.

title: Your AI Forgets Everything. Here's How I Fixed It.
excerpt: Every new session, your AI wakes up a stranger. How I gave mine persistent memory with two systems, Hindsight for technical facts, Ib for relational context, so it stops forgetting between conversations.
